name: token-proctor
description: Prompt validation, model routing, and token/turn cost projection for GitHub Copilot and other token-priced LLM plans.

chatParticipants.name: proctor
chatParticipants.description: Validate prompt, route to best model, project turns and token cost.

commands.name: route
commands.description: Pick the best model for this prompt

commands.name: validate
commands.description: Score prompt completeness; ask follow-ups if weak

commands.name: cost
commands.description: Estimate tokens, turns and $ for this prompt

commands.name: confirm
commands.description: Confirm the previous recommendation and forward to the chosen model

commands.name: cancel
commands.description: Cancel a pending confirmation — no model will be called

tokenProctor.completenessThreshold.description: Below this score, Token Proctor asks follow-up questions instead of invoking a model.

tokenProctor.autoForward.description: After routing, forward the prompt to the chosen model and stream the answer.

tokenProctor.requireConfirmation.description: Show the recommended model and wait for user confirmation (Accept & hand off button or /confirm) before calling the model.

tokenProctor.handoffToCopilot.description: When confirmed, hand the prompt off to the GitHub Copilot agent (which has file-edit tools) instead of streaming a plain text answer from vscode.lm. User should pick the recommended model in the chat model picker first.

tokenProctor.handoffParticipant.description: Chat participant id to hand off to when handoffToCopilot is enabled. Defaults to the GitHub Copilot agent.

tokenProctor.preferCheap.description: Bias routing toward the cheapest model that clears the quality bar.

tokenProctor.optimizeFor.markdownDescription:
How to weigh cost axes in routing:
- `tokens` — **default** — minimize $/M token price (classic); best for one-shot tasks.
- `turns` — minimize premium-multiplier × turns; best when agent loops run many turns (e.g. `code_large`, `agentic`).
- `balanced` — weighted compromise between the two.

tokenProctor.exactTokenCounts.description: Use js-tiktoken for exact token counts. Falls back to a chars/4 heuristic if unavailable.

tokenProctor.llmJudge.enabled.description: When rule-based classifier confidence is low, escalate to an LLM judge that auto-picks the cheapest available model via vscode.lm.

tokenProctor.llmJudge.confidenceThreshol.description: Rule-based confidence below this triggers the LLM-judge. Default 0.85 = judge is consulted on most prompts. 0 = always consult, 1 = never.
